title: pteridium latiusculum subsp. japonicum
intro: Pteridium latiusculum subsp. japonicum, also known as the Japanese bracken, is a subspecies of fern native to East Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and tall stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, which can be attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Pteridium latiusculum subsp. japonicum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
